title: Cyclopogon alpestris
intro: Cyclopogon alpestris, also known as the mountain Cyclopogon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Cyclopogon alpestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support the treatment of various health conditions, including arthritis and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyclopogon alpestris is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Orchidaceae and the genus Cyclopogon. It belongs to the order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes asparagus and lilies. The family Orchidaceae is the largest family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. This plant is further classified into the subfamily Orchidoideae, which is a group of orchids that are terrestrial and non-epiphytic. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and any recent revisions to the plant's taxonomy.
